value_chain_position: Crypto-native exchange, custodian, and financial infrastructure layer — charges trading fees, earns interest on USDC stablecoin float, and sells API/staking infrastructure to institutional and retail participants.
relative_comps: Coinbase is the dominant US-regulated crypto exchange and the largest publicly listed pure-play crypto infrastructure company. FCF margin of 48% in an up-cycle reflects extreme operating leverage on volume. Revenue is highly volatile: 3-year CAGR of 31% but YoY growth of -17% underscores the crypto-cycle dependency. Robinhood (HOOD) is a growing competitive threat with lower fees; CME and ICE are institutionally dominant in crypto derivatives. The fintech_rails fit is genuine — Coinbase earns exchange/clearing fees structurally similar to a securities exchange, but the underlying asset (crypto) is far more volatile than equities. The Base L2 blockchain and USDC custody move Coinbase toward a true toll-road infrastructure model.
